macro_geo.headline: Fed Policy Tightening Amid Sticky Inflation
macro_geo.insight: With CPI at 327.46 and Fed Funds at 3.64%, inflationary pressures remain elevated, signaled by a high VIX of 27.4. Expect the Fed to maintain or hike rates further in Q2 2026 to curb persistent price growth, risking slower economic activity.

macro_geo.headline: US-China Tariff Escalation Looms
macro_geo.insight: Rising geopolitical tensions suggest a potential renewal or increase in US tariffs on Chinese goods by mid-2026, driven by domestic political pressure. This could disrupt global supply chains and elevate costs, further fueling inflation.

macro_geo.headline: Energy Sector Volatility Impacts Global Markets
macro_geo.insight: Geopolitical instability in key oil-producing regions is likely to keep energy prices volatile, with Brent crude futures signaling upward pressure. Combined with high VIX, this adds risk to global equity markets, especially in energy-dependent economies.

regulation.headline: EU AI Act Finalized with Stricter Compliance Deadlines for High-Risk AI Systems
regulation.insight: On March 26, 2026, the European Union has officially finalized amendments to the EU AI Act, accelerating compliance deadlines for high-risk AI systems to mid-2027. The updated regulation mandates transparency in AI model training data and imposes fines up to 7% of global annual revenue for non-compliance. This signals a tightening grip on AI developers, particularly impacting US-based tech giants operating in the EU market, who must now prioritize alignment with these stringent rules or risk severe penalties.

regulation.headline: US Congress Introduces Bipartisan Crypto Regulation Bill Targeting Stablecoins
regulation.insight: A bipartisan group in the US Congress introduced a new bill on March 25, 2026, aimed at regulating stablecoins under the oversight of the SEC and the Federal Reserve. The legislation requires issuers to maintain 1:1 asset backing and submit to regular audits, addressing concerns over market stability post recent crypto volatility. This move indicates a potential shift toward federal-level clarity in crypto markets, though passage remains uncertain amid ongoing debates over SEC jurisdiction versus innovation stifling.

regulation.headline: What perks? Lawmakers play down special treatment as airport lines grow
House Republicans huddle with Johnson to plot party-line package
John Thune says he's aiming to land DHS deal Thursday

hot_takes.take: AI is overhyped in 2026—everyone’s chasing generative models, but the real money is in hyper-specialized, boring industrial AI. Think optimizing factory lines, not writing poetry. Big tech is distracted, so niche players will crush it.

Markets are sleepwalking into a correction by Q3 2026. Central banks are out of ammo, inflation’s sticky, and tech valuations are a house of cards. Everyone’s ‘buy the dip’ mentality is about to get torched.

Crypto’s done as a speculative asset class—Bitcoin’s just digital gold now, and altcoins are 99% scams. The ‘Web3 revolution’ narrative is dead; regulators will choke what’s left. Sell the hype, keep a tiny stack for hedging.

Macro’s bleak: China’s slowdown is worse than reported, and it’s dragging global growth into the gutter. Everyone’s betting on a US soft landing, but a hard recession is coming by 2027. Commodities are your only safe bet.
